skill.name: sonoscli
skill.summary: CLI skill for managing Sonos speakers on local network with playback and grouping controls.
skill.author: aiskillstore

security_audit.summary: Official skill from anthropics/skills repository. Uses sonoscli binary for local network Sonos speaker control. External commands and network access are expected and documented. No malicious patterns detected. Pre-reviewed by Anthropic.

content.user_title: Control Sonos Speakers with CLI Commands
content.value_statement: Managing Sonos speakers across your home network requires precise commands and troubleshooting knowledge. This skill enables Claude to discover speakers, control playback, adjust volume, and manage speaker groups using the sonoscli tool.
actual_capabilities: Discover Sonos speakers on the local network using SSDP protocol
Control playback state including play, pause, and stop commands
Adjust speaker volume levels and retrieve current status
Manage speaker groups with join, unjoin, party, and solo modes
List and open favorites, manage playback queue
Search Spotify tracks via SMAPI service integration
limitations: Requires sonoscli binary installed on the system before use
Network access required for local speaker discovery and control
Spotify search requires optional SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET credentials
MacOS requires Local Network privacy permission for direct mode operation

use_cases.title: Home Audio Automation
use_cases.description: Automate daily music routines by scheduling playback, adjusting volume based on time of day, and managing multi-room audio zones.

use_cases.title: Office Environment Control
use_cases.description: Manage background music across office spaces, coordinate announcements, and handle audio for meetings and events.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Support
use_cases.description: Enable voice-controlled speaker management for users with mobility limitations through natural language commands.

prompt_templates.title: Discover Available Speakers
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all Sonos speakers on my network and show me their current status.

prompt_templates.title: Control Playback
prompt_templates.prompt: Pause the music in the Kitchen and set the volume to 25 percent.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Speaker Groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Group the Living Room and Dining Room speakers together, then play jazz music in that zone.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Search and Queue
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Spotify for classic rock playlists, add the top result to the queue, and start playing it on all grouped speakers.

output_examples.output: Found 3 Sonos speakers: Kitchen (192.168.1.101), Living Room (192.168.1.102), Bedroom (192.168.1.103). All speakers are online and ready for control.
Kitchen speaker status: Playing 'Morning Jazz' at volume 45. Volume adjusted to 30. Track continues playing without interruption.
Group created: Kitchen and Living Room are now synchronized. Party mode activated. Audio will play simultaneously on both speakers.

best_practices: Always verify speaker availability before issuing playback commands to prevent errors
Use specific speaker names with the --name flag to target individual devices accurately
Specify IP addresses directly when SSDP discovery fails on complex network configurations
anti_patterns: Do not issue rapid volume changes in succession as this may cause audio spikes
Avoid grouping speakers across different network subnets without proper routing
Do not assume speaker names without verifying via discover command first

faq.question: How do I install the sonoscli binary?
faq.answer: Install via Go with: go install github.com/steipete/sonoscli/cmd/sonos@latest. The binary will be available in your GOPATH bin directory.

faq.question: Why does discover return no route to host error?
faq.answer: This indicates a network permission issue. On MacOS, enable Local Network access in Settings > Privacy & Security for your terminal or IDE application.

faq.question: Can I control speakers without knowing their IP addresses?
faq.answer: Yes, the discover command finds all speakers automatically. Use speaker names with the --name flag for subsequent commands.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Spotify?
faq.answer: Yes, Spotify search works via SMAPI. For full Spotify Web API features, set SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables.

faq.question: How do I ungroup speakers that were previously paired?
faq.answer: Use the command sonos group unjoin --name SpeakerName to remove a speaker from its current group.

faq.question: What network ports does sonoscli use?
faq.answer: Sonos uses UDP port 1900 for SSDP discovery and various ephemeral ports for control communication. Ensure these are not blocked by firewalls.
